{{Short description|Particle with half-integer spin obeying the Pauli exclusion principle}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Fermion
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Quantum mechanics]], [[Spin (physics)]], [[Pauli exclusion principle]], [[Identical particles]], [[Fermi–Dirac statistics]]
| examples = [[Electron]], [[Proton]], [[Neutron]], [[Quark]], [[Neutrino]]
}}

In [[particle physics]], a '''fermion''' is a [[subatomic particle]] that follows [[Fermi–Dirac statistics]] and has half-integer [[spin (physics)|spin]] (<math>1/2, 3/2, 5/2, \ldots</math>). Fermions are named after [[Enrico Fermi]]. They obey the [[Pauli exclusion principle]]: no two identical fermions can occupy the same [[quantum state]] simultaneously.

== Classification ==
Fermions fall into two categories:

=== Elementary fermions ===
In the [[Standard Model]], elementary fermions are the fundamental building blocks of matter:
* '''[[Quark]]s''' — six flavours: up, down, charm, strange, top, bottom
* '''[[Lepton]]s''' — electron, muon, tauon, and their associated [[neutrino]]s

=== Composite fermions ===
* '''[[Baryon]]s''' — three quarks bound together: [[proton]]s, [[neutron]]s
* Some [[atom]]s, when their total spin is half-integer (e.g., [[helium-3]], <math>^3\text{He}</math>)

== Properties ==
* Half-integer [[spin (physics)|spin]]
* Antisymmetric [[wave function]] under particle exchange
* Obey [[Fermi–Dirac statistics]]
* Subject to the [[Pauli exclusion principle]]
* At low temperatures, form [[degenerate matter]]: the [[Fermi gas]]

== Fermi–Dirac statistics ==
{{Main|Fermi–Dirac statistics}}
The average occupancy of a single-particle state with energy <math>E</math> is:
:<math>\langle n \rangle = \frac{1}{e^{(E - \mu)/k_B T} + 1}</math>

where <math>\mu</math> is the [[chemical potential]] ([[Fermi energy]] at <math>T = 0</math>).

== Degeneracy pressure ==
The Pauli exclusion principle gives rise to [[degeneracy pressure]], which supports:
* [[White dwarf]]s — [[electron degeneracy pressure]]
* [[Neutron star]]s — neutron degeneracy pressure
* [[Electron]]s in [[atom]]s — preventing collapse of atoms

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Boson]]
* [[Fermi–Dirac statistics]]
* [[Identical particles]]
* [[Pauli exclusion principle]]
* [[Spin (physics)]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Fermions| ]]
[[Category:Quantum field theory]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
[[Category:Statistical mechanics]]
